---
title: "D945GCLF2 no fan control"
date: 2010-05-11
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by pascalh on 2010-05-11
Hi,

I am trying to control fan speed on a D945GCLF2 motherboard in Ubuntu 10.04.
After enabling fan control in the BIOS, the only detected sensors are:

```
cat /etc/modules 
# /etc/modules: kernel modules to load at boot time.
#
# This file contains the names of kernel modules that should be loaded
# at boot time, one per line. Lines beginning with "#" are ignored.

lp


# Generated by sensors-detect on Mon May 10 19:27:43 2010
# Chip drivers
coretemp
smsc47m1
```This results in inaccurate sensor information (it seems):

```
sensors
coretemp-isa-0000
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 0:       +0.0°C  (crit = +90.0°C)                  

coretemp-isa-0001
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 1:       +3.0°C  (crit = +90.0°C)                  

coretemp-isa-0002
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 2:       +1.0°C  (crit = +90.0°C)                  

coretemp-isa-0003
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 3:       +3.0°C  (crit = +90.0°C)
```Some threads suggest that fan control is possible on this board:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/archive/index.php/t-948830.html](http://ubuntuforums.org/archive/index.php/t-948830.html)
I tried copying the sensor info into /etc/modules and rebooted with no success.

I also found:
[http://tech.akom.net/archives/9-Gentoo-Linux-on-D945GCLF-with-Atom-230.html](http://tech.akom.net/archives/9-Gentoo-Linux-on-D945GCLF-with-Atom-230.html)

which mentions "enabling  **the Intel 82801 (ICH)** i2c module (i2c-i801)" in the kernel, which I don't know how to do...

Finally this page also suggests there are other sensors than the ones detected on my setup:
[http://en.gentoo-wiki.com/wiki/Intel_D945GCLF2](http://en.gentoo-wiki.com/wiki/Intel_D945GCLF2)

Any idea how I could load/detect/enable those sensors?

Thanks.

### Post by Dreamsmaster on 2010-06-18
I have the same problem!!!

When i run "sensors-detect" i found two sensor:


> Driver `smsc47m1':
  * ISA bus, address 0x680
    Chip `SMSC LPC47M15x/192/997 Super IO Fan Sensors' (confidence: 9)

Driver `coretemp':
  * Chip `Intel Atom thermal sensor' (confidence: 9)

To load everything that is needed, add this to /etc/modules:
#----cut here----
# Chip drivers
coretemp
smsc47m1
#----cut here----But if i try to launch command:

      ```
modprobe smsc47m1
```i retrieve the messagge:

> FATAL: Error inserting smsc47m1 (/lib/modules/2.6.35-999-generic/kernel/drivers/hwmon/smsc47m1.ko): Device or resource busyAnyone have a solution?

---

### Post by Dreamsmaster on 2010-07-07
Hi every all!
I have partially solved!
With the last version on ubuntu (9.10) and with the update of the version 9.04 of GRUB the option
```
acpi_enforce_resources=lax
```is to write on the file /etc/default/grub (GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=acpi_enforce_resources=lax) and not on the older conf file of the previous version of GRUB (/boot/grub/menu.lst)

Then update grub ```
sudo update-grub
```Now when i launch sensors  retrieve the following

```
dreamsmaster@Eracle:~$ sensors
coretemp-isa-0000
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 0:       +7.0°C  (crit = +90.0°C)                  

coretemp-isa-0001
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 1:       +7.0°C  (crit = +90.0°C)                  

smsc47m1-isa-0680
Adapter: ISA adapter
fan1:       5120 RPM  (min = 1280 RPM, div = 4)
fan2:       6642 RPM  (min = 1280 RPM, div = 4)

```Now i have the correct indication of the speed of the fans but i have incorrect indication of the cpu temp....WORK IN PROGRESS

PS: Sorry for my bad english!! ;)

---
